title: Luria Bertani Broth and agarized
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"65p01","text":"Recipe and protocol for Luria Bertani broth (Miller version) preparation, an optional agarization step is provided. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Weight ingredients: Tryptone powder (technical grade) 10 g/L Yeast extract 5 g/L NaCl (Miller recipe) 10 g/L Milli Q or Tap water to volume 1L.

Add all the solid ingredients to a plastic or glass becker, along with 600-700mL of water to dissolve the ingredients. Its facultative to measure Water with a cilinder. Its facultative to add autoclavable components (vitamins, antibiotics etc.)
Make up to final 1L volume with remaining water and further mix.

Pour the content into a Duran to autoclave the mixture. Do not fill the whole bottle, leave some headspace (1/4 of total volume HS). Do not seal the bottle, leave the top half-round open. Remember to place autoclave-tape over your bottles. Autoclave setting: 121°C for 15min, 15psi/1bar
Take note of the position of YourFavouriteIngredients and take them from the reagent-room.
Add 15 g/L Agar microbiology grade along with 100mL of water to "dissolve" the agar. It will settle to the bottom due to low solubilization below 50°C, it will be mixed during autoclaving step.
creator: Marta Puddu
